channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Dow builds on 53,000 record, chip stocks fall as investors rotate bets: Live updates
item.description: Monday night's action follows a winning session on Wall Street.

item.title: Trump arrives in Turkey as NATO is strained by Russian attacks, U.S. impatience
item.description: Trump frequently vented about NATO members' refusal to heed U.S. calls for help clearing the Strait of Hormuz during its campaign against Iran.

item.title: Rivian stock falls 13% as company sells 75 million shares to raise capital
item.description: The capital raise occurred during extended hours trading following Rivian shares increasing 8.1% on Monday after increasing 19.2% last week.

item.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russia’s largest oil refinery as Zelenskyy says Siberia now ‘within reach’
item.description: The attack underscores Kyiv's enhanced long-range drone capabilities and comes on the eve of a crucial NATO summit.

item.title: One SpaceX analyst sees Musk's space company quintupling to $800
item.description: Raymond James and other shops on the Street released their calls on SpaceX on Tuesday as the 25-day quiet period for underwriters of its IPO ended.

item.title: Toyota to invest $3.6 billion to move Tacoma pickup truck production from Mexico to Texas
item.description: Toyota said it is investing $3.6 billion to move production of the Tacoma midsize pickup truck from Mexico to its San Antonio manufacturing campus in Texas.

item.title: Oil prices edge higher after report of Iranian attack on commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Brent and U.S. crude futures rose after a report of an Iranian attack on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Major banks give their first ratings on SpaceX and they're quite bullish: 'Apex of civilizational ambition'
item.description: Financial underwriters of SpaceX's IPO are now free to release their equity research.

item.title: Billionaire John Arnold commits $2.6 million to study online sports betting risk
item.description: John Arnold, who runs Arnold Ventures with his wife, Laura, is investing $2.6 million for university research into potential harms of online sports betting.

item.title: Far more real estate agents now report seeing a balanced market, CNBC Housing Market Survey finds
item.description: Agents who reported at least one price cut to active listings dropped dramatically from prior surveys.

item.title: Chinese lidar maker with Nvidia ties accused of being cyber risk for U.S.
item.description: Hesai Technology was blacklisted as a national security threat in 2024 by the U.S. Department of Defense, which designated Hesai as a Chinese military entity.

item.title: George E. Johnson, founder of pioneering Black hair products company, dead at 99
item.description: George E. Johnson, the pioneering Chicago entrepreneur whose eponymous company transformed Black hair care in the U.S. died on Monday at age 99.

item.title: Graham Platner denies sex assault claim as Democrats urge him to quit Maine Senate race
item.description: Graham Platner hopes to unseat Republican Sen. Susan Collins, but the Democrat's campaign is reeling from a bombshell allegation by Jenny Racicot.

item.title: Trump Accounts are live. Here's how some families plan to use them
item.description: As Trump Accounts launch, millions of parents are figuring out how the new investment accounts fit with their family's financial goals.

item.title: Here are Tuesday's biggest analyst calls: SpaceX, Nvidia, Apple, Figma, Shopify, First Solar, Ferrari & more
item.description: Here are Tuesday's biggest calls on Wall Street.

item.title: World Cup in photos: Belgium beats U.S. 4-1 after Balogun controversy, Ronaldo exits
item.description: The U.S. is out of the World Cup, losing 4-1 to Belgium in the Round of 16 in a match blemished by suspension of Folarin Balogun's red card. Ronaldo exits.

item.title: Hanwha Ocean shares sink 23% as it loses bid to build Canada's next fleet of submarines
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney announced Monday that Germany's Thyssenkrupp Marine Systems would be the preferred supplier for delivering the submarines.

item.title: Google backs nuclear fusion startup targeting Europe’s first commercial power plant
item.description: Proxima Fusion has raised $468 million as it looks to move towards commercializing the promising but infamously difficult technical challenge of nuclear fusion.

item.title: China's Alibaba bans Anthropic AI for employees after 'distillation attack' accusation
item.description: Chinese e-commerce giant Alibaba has put Anthropic's Claude Code on a high-risk software list.

item.title: Chinese AI models are gaining ground with U.S. companies as OpenAI, Anthropic costs surge
item.description: Recent model releases from Chinese companies including DeepSeek and Z.ai are seen by many as highly competitive compared to leading U.S. frontier systems.

item.title: Why oil investors fear the next toll fight could be the Strait of Malacca
item.description: The prospect of fees to transit the Strait of Hormuz has sparked alarm, not least by investors who fear it could be replicated in other maritime corridors.

item.title: These 13 states don't tax Social Security, 401(k) accounts, IRAs or pensions
item.description: Would you move to avoid paying taxes on your retirement nest egg? Find out which states don't tax Social Security, 401(k) accounts and more.

item.title: Student loan servicers begin 90-day countdown for borrowers to leave SAVE plan
item.description: Student loan servicers have begun alerting borrowers that they have 90 days to leave the Biden-era SAVE plan. Here's what comes next.

item.title: India fashion retailer Trent tumbles 11% as revenue growth disappoints
item.description: Shares of one of India's biggest fashion retailers, Trent, fell over 10% after the company reported weak earnings growth.

item.title: Klarna seeks U.S. bank charter in latest push beyond buy now, pay later
item.description: Klarna joins a wave of fintech and crypto firms seeking entry to the traditional banking system.

item.title: To future-proof your career in the age of AI, do 3 simple things: You'll 'remain valuable,' says expert
item.description: Dan Schawbel, who's spent over a decade researching how technology reshapes careers, says employees who do these 3 things in the age of AI will "come out ahead."

item.title: Here's how SpaceX's Nasdaq-100 inclusion might affect options pricing
item.description: About half-a-million SpaceX options traded by midday Monday, a little below the average since inception.

item.title: Microsoft cuts 4,800 jobs, as Xbox unit downsizes and plans to spin off four gaming studios
item.description: Microsoft is cutting jobs in its commercial business and its Xbox gaming group, where revenue has been shrinking.

item.title: Rivian, Tesla and the 'Holly Index': How to trade the new EV Main Street battle
item.description: Mike Khouw gives a trade on Rivian and Tesla, informed by his wife's recent auto purchase.

item.title: SpaceX President Gwynne Shotwell to donate stock to Trump Accounts
item.description: President Donald Trump told CNBC's Joe Kernen last week that he thought SpaceX CEO Elon Musk would donate company stock to the program.
